name: County Durham (United Kingdom)
description: A blue field bearing a yellow cross of Saint Cuthbert (a cross with each arm ending in four small balls / pommels).
history: Based on the cross of Saint Cuthbert (the patron saint of Durham); chosen in a public competition organised by Durham County Council and registered with the Flag Institute on 21 November 2013.

features.symbolism: the cross of Saint Cuthbert, patron saint of County Durham; the four balls on each arm refer to the cross's heraldic 'pommetty' form
